user_query:
I exported snapshots of my recent OS activity as pipe-separated text. Please read all three files under input/ and produce a normalized, metrics-rich summary:

Inputs (PSV with headers on the first line):
- input/recent_files.txt
- input/programs.txt
- input/processes.txt

Do the following and write all outputs under output/ as specified:
1) Normalize recent files to CSV.
   - Read input/recent_files.txt (header: "Filename|Path|Type|Last Edited Time").
   - Create output/normalized/recent_files.csv with header exactly "filename,path,type,last_edited_iso".
   - For each row, convert the "Last Edited Time" value from "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" to ISO 8601 "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS" and place it in last_edited_iso.
   - Preserve the original row order and include all rows.

2) Normalize installed programs to JSON.
   - Read input/programs.txt (header: "Name|Publisher|Version|Install Location").
   - Create output/normalized/programs.json containing an array of objects with keys exactly: name, publisher, version, install_location.
   - Sort the array by name ascending (A–Z) before writing.

3) Compute summary metrics to JSON.
   - Read all three inputs.
   - Create output/metrics/summary.json with keys:
     - recent_files_count: total number of rows in recent_files.txt.
     - unique_extensions: count of distinct file extensions from the Filename column (rule: extension = substring after the last dot; if there is no dot, use empty string; treat extensions case-insensitively, convert to lowercase).
     - by_extension: an object mapping each lowercase extension to its count.
     - running_processes_count: total number of rows in processes.txt.
     - installed_programs_count: total number of rows in programs.txt.
     - top_processes: an array of the top 3 processes by Percent Processor Time (descending), each with keys exactly pid (number), name (string), and cpu (number). Parse CPU as a number, not a string.

4) List running processes that don’t have an exact name match in installed programs.
   - Compare each process Name in input/processes.txt to the set of installed program Name values in input/programs.txt using a case-insensitive exact string comparison.
   - If a process name does not exactly match any installed program name (ignoring case), include that process name in a unique set.
   - Write the result as a JSON array (strings only) sorted ascending to output/checks/processes_not_installed.json.

Do not include extra fields beyond what is specified. Make sure all paths you write are exactly as listed above.
